key_insight: EVMDOT turns dynamic optimal transport into an unconstrained energetic variational problem that can recover both velocity fields and energy landscapes directly from density time-series data.
engineering_relevance: This gives engineers a data-driven way to infer latent dynamics and potential landscapes in complex systems, with applications in physics-informed modeling, biology, and medical trajectory analysis.
method: Uses the flow map to automatically satisfy the continuity equation.
Combines least action and maximum dissipation principles to balance conservative and dissipative forces.
Applies the framework to Fokker–Planck dynamics to reconstruct energy and Waddington landscapes from observed densities.
evidence_signal: The paper reports numerical experiments showing robust landscape reconstruction across data quantity–quality trade-offs and an application to ADNI that separates cognitively normal and Alzheimer's disease states with a transition path.
watch_item: Reconstruction quality still depends on the amount and reliability of time-series density data, so sparse or noisy observations may limit identifiability.
paper_title: Learning the Energy Landscapes of Dynamical Systems via Energetic Variational Optimal Transport under Data Quantity--Quality Trade-offs
